response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with 15+ years of experience, but the role demands deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation, and his only professional C++ work was a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 — C++ does not even appear in his skills list. His recent decade centers on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms, with no exposure to MQTT, message brokers at industrial scale, IEC 62443, or Secure Development Lifecycle practices. His low-latency DLang systems work shows transferable performance-engineering instincts, but the core technical match is too thin for a senior C++ role in this domain.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Real high-performance systems experience (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests/sec with async I/O and fibers)
Strong engineering-culture credentials: TDD, testing frameworks authorship, mentoring, workshops, and open-source leadership
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~7 months, ended 2015) and C++ is absent from his own skills list
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT/OPC UA/Modbus), or IEC 62443 / SDL security background
Career trajectory is firmly web/full-stack TypeScript/Node, plus an unexplained ~8-month employment gap in 2020

key_factors.factor: Industrial automation and security standards exposure
